Configures an external toolchain for CMake with specified compiler settings.

# Purpose
This configuration file is used in a CMake build system to set up the environment for cross-compilation. It includes the `CMakeForceCompiler` module, which allows the specification of a compiler that is not automatically detected by CMake. The `CMAKE_SYSTEM_NAME` is set to `EXTERNAL`, indicating that the build is targeting an external system. The `CMAKE_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR` is assigned the value of the variable `EXTERNAL_CMAKE_SYSTEM_PROCESSOR`, which specifies the processor architecture of the target system. The `CMAKE_FORCE_C_COMPILER` command is used to explicitly set the C compiler and its identification, using the variables `EXTERNAL_CMAKE_C_COMPILER` and `EXTERNAL_CMAKE_C_COMPILER_ID`, ensuring that the specified compiler is used for the build process.
